claims.summary: The 48D semantic contract carries narrative-arc and tail-participation features beyond the legacy 32D surface.
claims.disposition: Verified the registered representation contract and compatibility projection without treating width as proof of felt improvement.

claims.summary: The entropy-gated vibrancy transition should remain smooth rather than switch abruptly.
claims.disposition: Verified the smoothstep implementation and existing boundary/no-pop regression coverage.

claims.summary: Clamp pressure may still feel flattening when high-entropy tail energy approaches its ceiling.
claims.disposition: Preserved as unscored felt friction; mechanical clamp safety does not resolve the report.

claims.summary: High-entropy clamp occupancy and narrative-lane movement should be compared against lower-entropy baselines.
claims.disposition: Routed to the existing capture-first codec campaigns using lane energy, headroom, clamp occupancy, and representation-loss receipts only.

claims.summary: The live feature ceiling should rise dynamically with vibrancy.
claims.disposition: Held for Tier 5 operator approval because codec ceilings and gain alter live semantic transport.
